calendar.description: Senate Government Operations and Political Subdivisions Hearing

general_provisions: This bill amends provisions relating to elections.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>defines terms;<hr><ltbullet>modifies the conditions and requirements for a ballot to list a candidate using a variation of the candidate's name or a nickname;<hr><ltbullet>modifies, consolidates, recodifies, and adds provisions relating to:<hr><ltbullet1>notices of offices to be filled at upcoming elections;<hr><ltbullet1>notices of candidate filing periods;<hr><ltbullet1>notices of candidates to be included on a ballot and other instructions relating to a ballot;<hr><ltbullet1>notices of election; and<hr><ltbullet1>other notices relating to elections;<hr><ltbullet>modifies certain declaration of candidacy periods:<hr><ltbullet1>to coincide with the declaration of candidacy periods for municipal primary elections and municipal general elections; and<hr><ltbullet1>to make declaration of candidacy periods for all registered political parties the same, regardless of whether the registered political party is a qualified political party;<hr><ltbullet>beginning on January 1, 2027, prohibits a county clerk's or election officer's name from:<hr><ltbullet1>inclusion in the official endorsement for a ballot;<hr><ltbullet1>inclusion on a return envelope for a ballot; or<hr><ltbullet1>inclusion on a ballot, except to the extent that the county clerk or election officer is listed as a candidate on the ballot;<hr><ltbullet>modifies certain deadlines set at a specific date to instead occur on a business day;<hr><ltbullet>modifies and consolidates provisions relating to removing the names of deceased individuals from the list of registered voters;<hr><ltbullet>modifies requirements relating to the storage of election material, ballots, and election returns;<hr><ltbullet>modifies the deadline to file an objection to a declaration of candidacy;<hr><ltbullet>modifies and removes inconsistent provisions relating to participation in a regular primary election;<hr><ltbullet>grants rulemaking authority to the Office of the Lieutenant Governor to regulate the use and application of the Great Seal of the State of Utah or a replica of the seal;<hr><ltbullet>modifies and clarifies provisions relating to the crime of illegally using or defacing the Great Seal of the State of Utah; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical and conforming changes.
